sanskrit: तमस्त्वज्ञानजं विद्धि मोहनं सर्वदेहिनाम्। प्रमादालस्यनिद्राभिस्तन्मिबध्नाति भारत॥
transliteration: tamas tv ajñāna-jaṁ viddhi mohanaṁ sarva-dehinām | pramādālāsya-nidrābhis tan nibadhnāti bhārata ||
literal_meaning: Know tamas to be born of ignorance, deluding all embodied beings; through heedlessness, laziness and sleep it binds them, O Bharata.
contextual_meaning: The psychological field of tamas is a dense fog that narcotizes perception, replacing curiosity with apathy, effort with paralysis, and wakefulness with compulsive dormancy.
psychological_state: Hypo-aroused dissociation: cortical shutdown, dopaminergic blunting, behavioral shutdown maintained by secondary gains of avoidance.
trigger_scenario: Scrolling mindlessly for hours, postponing urgent tasks while feeling a heavy, glue-like fatigue that makes even standing up seem Herculean.
anchor_text: Wake up from laziness and ignorance to reclaim your clarity and purpose.
